content.content:
1	# type-vocabulary.tsv — canonical owners for cross-crate Rust types.
2	#
3	# Format:
4	#   <name> <kind> <owner-path> <mode> <notes>
5	#
6	# mode:
7	#   enforce  hook fails if a modified file defines this name outside owner-path
8	#   audit    audit reports it, but changed-file hook does not fail
9	#
10	# Keep this list small and intentional. It is not every Rust type in the
11	# workspace; it is the cross-cutting vocabulary whose duplication fragments the
12	# architecture.
13	
14	LuaState        struct  crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs       enforce  canonical interpreter state
15	GlobalState     struct  crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs       enforce  canonical shared VM state
16	CallInfo        struct  crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs       enforce  canonical call frame record
17	LuaValue        enum    crates/lua-types/src/value.rs    enforce  canonical TValue replacement
18	LuaError        enum    crates/lua-types/src/error.rs    enforce  canonical error type
19	LuaString       struct  crates/lua-types/src/string.rs   audit    currently duplicated in lua-vm; reconcile before enforce
20	LuaTable        struct  crates/lua-vm/src/table.rs       audit    currently duplicated/placeheld; reconcile before enforce
21	LuaProto        struct  crates/lua-types/src/proto.rs    audit    currently duplicated/placeheld; reconcile before enforce
22	LuaClosure      enum    crates/lua-types/src/closure.rs  audit    currently duplicated/placeheld; reconcile before enforce
23	UpVal           enum    crates/lua-types/src/upval.rs    audit    canonical upvalue shape
24	LuaUserData     struct  crates/lua-types/src/userdata.rs enforce  canonical userdata type
25	LuaStatus       enum    crates/lua-types/src/status.rs   enforce  canonical status enum
26	StackIdx        struct  crates/lua-types/src/lib.rs      enforce  canonical stack index
27	CallInfoIdx     struct  crates/lua-types/src/lib.rs      enforce  canonical call-info index
28	OpCode          enum    crates/lua-code/src/opcodes.rs   audit    currently duplicated/placeheld; reconcile before enforce
29	Instruction     struct  crates/lua-code/src/opcodes.rs   audit    currently duplicated/placeheld; reconcile before enforce
30

file.content:
# type-vocabulary.tsv — canonical owners for cross-crate Rust types.
#
# Format:
#   <name> <kind> <owner-path> <mode> <notes>
#
# mode:
#   enforce  hook fails if a modified file defines this name outside owner-path
#   audit    audit reports it, but changed-file hook does not fail
#
# Keep this list small and intentional. It is not every Rust type in the
# workspace; it is the cross-cutting vocabulary whose duplication fragments the
# architecture.

LuaState        struct  crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs       enforce  canonical interpreter state
GlobalState     struct  crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs       enforce  canonical shared VM state
CallInfo        struct  crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs       enforce  canonical call frame record
LuaValue        enum    crates/lua-types/src/value.rs    enforce  canonical TValue replacement
LuaError        enum    crates/lua-types/src/error.rs    enforce  canonical error type
LuaString       struct  crates/lua-types/src/string.rs   audit    currently duplicated in lua-vm; reconcile before enforce
LuaTable        struct  crates/lua-vm/src/table.rs       audit    currently duplicated/placeheld; reconcile before enforce
LuaProto        struct  crates/lua-types/src/proto.rs    audit    currently duplicated/placeheld; reconcile before enforce
LuaClosure      enum    crates/lua-types/src/closure.rs  audit    currently duplicated/placeheld; reconcile before enforce
UpVal           enum    crates/lua-types/src/upval.rs    audit    canonical upvalue shape
LuaUserData     struct  crates/lua-types/src/userdata.rs enforce  canonical userdata type
LuaStatus       enum    crates/lua-types/src/status.rs   enforce  canonical status enum
StackIdx        struct  crates/lua-types/src/lib.rs      enforce  canonical stack index
CallInfoIdx     struct  crates/lua-types/src/lib.rs      enforce  canonical call-info index
OpCode          enum    crates/lua-code/src/opcodes.rs   audit    currently duplicated/placeheld; reconcile before enforce
Instruction     struct  crates/lua-code/src/opcodes.rs   audit    currently duplicated/placeheld; reconcile before enforce

2423 crates/lua-code/src/codegen.rs
    1074 crates/lua-code/src/opcodes.rs
      75 crates/lua-code/src/opcode_names.rs
    3572 total

1	//! Code generator for Lua 5.4.
2	//!
3	//! Ports `src/lcode.c` (1875 lines, ~60 functions) and the public interface
4	//! from `src/lcode.h`.
5	//!
6	//! Phase A: faithful logic translation — does not yet compile.
7	//!
8	//! TODO(port): FuncState.f is GcRef<LuaProto> per types.tsv but the code
9	//! generator mutates it directly.  Phase B must wrap LuaProto in
10	//! RefCell<LuaProto> (or similar) inside GcRef so that `fs.f.code`, `fs.f.k`,
11	//! etc. are mutable.  All `fs.f.<field>` accesses below assume direct mutability.
12	//!
13	//! TODO(port): LexState.L (the &mut LuaState back-pointer) was removed per
14	//! types.tsv.  Functions that call luaM_growvector / luaH_get / luaC_barrier /
15	//! luaO_rawarith carry an explicit `state: &mut LuaState` parameter here.
16	//! The caller (lua-parse) must thread it through.
17	
18	// C: /* $Id: lcode.c $ */
19	// C: #define lcode_c
20	// C: #define LUA_CORE
21	
22	use crate::opcodes::{
23	    Instruction, OpCode, OpMode,
24	    MAXARG_A, MAXARG_B, MAXARG_C, MAXARG_BX, MAXARG_AX, MAXARG_S_J,
25	    OFFSET_S_BX, OFFSET_S_J, OFFSET_S_C,
26	    NO_REG, MAXINDEXRK, LFIELDS_PER_FLUSH,
27	};
28	
29	use lua_types::error::LuaError;
30	use lua_types::value::LuaValue;
31	use lua_types::string::LuaString;
32	use lua_types::gc::GcRef;
33	use lua_types::tagmethod::TagMethod;
34	
35	// ─── PHASE B PLACEHOLDERS ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
36	//
37	// The following types live in `lua-parse` (`FuncState`, `ExprDesc`, `ExprKind`,
38	// `LexState`) and `lua-vm` (`LuaState`, `LuaTable`).  Importing them from those
39	// crates would create a dependency cycle (`lua-vm` depends on `lua-code`), so
40	// we keep stub shapes here in Phase B.  The real definitions land when those
41	// crates fully exist; the field/method signatures below are inferred from the
42	// concrete usage in `codegen.rs`.
43	
44	/// PHASE B: real definition lives in lua-types (`lua_types::proto::LuaProto`),
45	/// but it stores `Vec<lua_types::opcode::Instruction>` — a barebones u32 wrapper
46	/// without the `arg_a` / `set_opcode` / `is_in_top` / etc. methods that the
47	/// code generator calls.  Those methods live on the local
48	/// `crate::opcodes::Instruction` type.  Until the two `Instruction` types are
49	/// unified (Phase B), `FuncState` carries a local proto shape that uses the
50	/// methodful local `Instruction`.
51	pub struct LuaProto {
52	    pub numparams: u8,
53	    pub is_vararg: bool,
54	    pub maxstacksize: u8,
55	    pub k: Vec<LuaValue>,
56	    pub code: Vec<Instruction>,
57	    pub lineinfo: Vec<i8>,
58	    pub abslineinfo: Vec<AbsLineInfo>,
59	}
60	
61	/// PHASE B: mirrors `lua_types::proto::AbsLineInfo`; kept local so it pairs
62	/// with the local `LuaProto` placeholder above.
63	#[derive(Clone, Copy)]
64	pub struct AbsLineInfo {
65	    pub pc: i32,
66	    pub line: i32,
67	}
68	
69	/// PHASE B: real definition lives in lua-parse; placeholder shape inferred
70	/// from codegen usage.
71	pub struct FuncState {
72	    pub f: LuaProto,
73	    pub pc: i32,
74	    pub lasttarget: i32,
75	    pub previousline: i32,
76	    pub iwthabs: u8,
77	    pub nabslineinfo: i32,
78	    pub nactvar: u8,
79	    pub freereg: u8,
80	    pub nk: i32,
81	    pub needclose: bool,
82	}
83	
84	/// PHASE B: real definition lives in lua-parse; placeholder shape inferred
85	/// from codegen usage.  Mirrors the C `expdesc` struct (a tagged kind plus a
86	/// payload union); the union becomes a plain struct here so every variant's
87	/// fields are addressable.
88	pub struct ExprDesc {
89	    pub k: ExprKind,
90	    pub t: i32,
91	    pub f: i32,
92	    pub u: ExprDescU,
93	}
94	
95	impl Clone for ExprDesc {
96	    fn clone(&self) -> Self {
97	        ExprDesc {
98	            k: self.k,
99	            t: self.t,
100	            f: self.f,
101	            u: ExprDescU {
102	                info: self.u.info,
103	                ival: self.u.ival,
104	                nval: self.u.nval,
105	                strval: self.u.strval.clone(),
106	                var: self.u.var,
107	                ind: self.u.ind,
108	            },
109	        }
110	    }
111	}
112	
113	impl ExprDesc {
114	    /// C: `static const expdesc ef = {VKINT, {0}, NO_JUMP, NO_JUMP};`
115	    /// PHASE B: a zero `VKINT` literal used as a synthetic operand by the
116	    /// constant-folding helpers in `prefix()`.
117	    pub fn const_zero() -> Self {
118	        todo!("PHASE B: ExprDesc::const_zero — real shape requires GcRef<LuaString> default")
119	    }
120	}
121	
122	/// PHASE B: real definition lives in lua-parse; placeholder shape inferred
123	/// from codegen usage.  The C union becomes a struct: simultaneous fields are
124	/// harmless because Phase B will gate them by `ExprKind`.
125	pub struct ExprDescU {
126	    pub info: i32,
127	    pub ival: i64,
128	    pub nval: f64,
129	    pub strval: GcRef<LuaString>,
130	    pub var: ExprDescVar,
131	    pub ind: ExprDescInd,
132	}
133	
134	/// PHASE B: real definition lives in lua-parse; placeholder shape inferred
135	/// from codegen usage.
136	#[derive(Clone, Copy)]
137	pub struct ExprDescVar {
138	    pub ridx: u8,
139	}
140	
141	/// PHASE B: real definition lives in lua-parse; placeholder shape inferred
142	/// from codegen usage.
143	#[derive(Clone, Copy)]
144	pub struct ExprDescInd {
145	    pub t: u8,
146	    pub idx: i16,
147	}
148	
149	/// PHASE B: real definition lives in lua-parse; placeholder shape inferred
150	/// from codegen usage.  Variant set comes from the `ExprKind::V*` literals
151	/// referenced in `codegen.rs`.
152	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
153	pub enum ExprKind {
154	    VCall,
155	    VConst,
156	    VFalse,
157	    VIndexI,
158	    VIndexStr,
159	    VIndexUp,
160	    VIndexed,
161	    VJmp,
162	    VK,
163	    VKFlt,
164	    VKInt,
165	    VKStr,
166	    VLocal,
167	    VNil,
168	    VNonReloc,
169	    VReloc,
170	    VTrue,
171	    VUpVal,
172	    VVarArg,
173	}
174	
175	/// PHASE B: real definition lives in lua-parse; placeholder shape inferred
176	/// from codegen usage.
177	pub struct LexState {
178	    pub t: LexToken,
179	    pub dyd: Dyndata,
180	    pub lastline: i32,
181	    pub h: GcRef<LuaTable>,
182	}
183	
184	/// PHASE B: real definition lives in lua-parse; placeholder shape inferred
185	/// from codegen usage.
186	pub struct LexToken {
187	    pub token: i32,
188	}
189	
190	/// PHASE B: real definition lives in lua-parse; placeholder shape inferred
191	/// from codegen usage.
192	pub struct Dyndata {
193	    pub actvar: Vec<ActvarEntry>,
194	}
195	
196	/// PHASE B: real definition lives in lua-parse; placeholder shape inferred
197	/// from codegen usage.
198	pub struct ActvarEntry {
199	    pub k: LuaValue,
200	}
201	
202	/// PHASE B: real definition lives in lua-vm; placeholder shape inferred from
203	/// codegen usage (only `equal_obj` and `raw_arith` are called here).
204	pub struct LuaState {
205	    _private: (),
206	}
207	
208	impl LuaState {
209	    pub fn equal_obj(&self, _mt: Option<()>, _a: &LuaValue, _b: &LuaValue) -> bool {
210	        todo!("PHASE B: LuaState::equal_obj — lives in lua-vm")
211	    }
212	
213	    pub fn raw_arith(
214	        &mut self,
215	        _op: i32,
216	        _a: &LuaValue,
217	        _b: &LuaValue,
218	    ) -> Result<LuaValue, LuaError> {
219	        todo!("PHASE B: LuaState::raw_arith — lives in lua-vm")
220	    }
221	}
222	
223	/// PHASE B: the runtime table type lives in lua-vm; the existing
224	/// `lua_types::value::LuaTable` is a forward-decl with no methods.  We re-use
225	/// that type for `ls.h` so it can be `clone()`d into `LuaValue::Table`; the
226	/// `get` / `finish_set` operations are exposed here as free-function shims
227	/// (they will become real method calls in Phase B).
228	pub use lua_types::value::LuaTable;
229	
230	/// PHASE B: real impl lives in lua-vm (`LuaTable::get`).
231	fn lex_h_get(_table: &GcRef<LuaTable>, _state: &LuaState, _key: &LuaValue) -> LuaValue {
232	    todo!("PHASE B: LuaTable::get — lives in lua-vm")
233	}
234	
235	/// PHASE B: real impl lives in lua-vm (`LuaTable::finish_set`).
236	fn lex_h_finish_set(
237	    _table: &GcRef<LuaTable>,
238	    _state: &mut LuaState,
239	    _key: &LuaValue,
240	    _val: &LuaValue,
241	) -> Result<(), LuaError> {
242	    todo!("PHASE B: LuaTable::finish_set — lives in lua-vm")
243	}
244	
245	/// PHASE B: real definition for `TagMethod::from_delta` lives in lua-vm; this
246	/// shim performs the offset math locally so codegen can keep its
247	/// `binopr2_tm`-style ports.
248	#[inline]
249	fn tag_method_from_delta(base: TagMethod, delta: i32) -> TagMethod {
250	    let raw = (base as i32 + delta) as u8;
251	    use TagMethod::*;
252	    match raw {
253	        0  => Index,
254	        1  => NewIndex,
255	        2  => Gc,
256	        3  => Mode,
257	        4  => Len,
258	        5  => Eq,
259	        6  => Add,
260	        7  => Sub,
261	        8  => Mul,
262	        9  => Mod,
263	        10 => Pow,
264	        11 => Div,
265	        12 => Idiv,
266	        13 => Band,
267	        14 => Bor,
268	        15 => Bxor,
269	        16 => Shl,
270	        17 => Shr,
271	        18 => Unm,
272	        19 => Bnot,
273	        20 => Lt,
274	        21 => Le,
275	        22 => Concat,
276	        23 => Call,
277	        24 => Close,
278	        _  => todo!("PHASE B: TagMethod::from_delta out-of-range"),
279	    }
280	}
281	
282	/// PHASE B: real definition for `OpCode::from_delta` lives in lua-code/opcodes
283	/// but isn't wired up yet; this shim performs the offset cast.
284	#[inline]
285	fn opcode_from_delta(base: OpCode, delta: i32) -> OpCode {
286	    let raw = (base as i32 + delta) as u32;
287	    match OpCode::from_u32(raw) {
288	        Some(op) => op,
289	        None => todo!("PHASE B: OpCode::from_delta out-of-range"),
290	    }
291	}
292	
293	// ─── Constants (lcode.h + local) ──────────────────────────────────────────────
294	
295	// C: #define NO_JUMP (-1)
296	/// End-of-patch-list sentinel.  An invalid value both as an absolute address
297	/// and as a list link (would link an element to itself).
298	pub const NO_JUMP: i32 = -1;
299	
300	// C: #define MAXREGS 255

content.text:
Now I understand the picture. Let me make the change. The local LuaState struct should be replaced with `pub use lua_vm::state::LuaState;`. Then I need to deal with the `raw_arith` and `equal_obj` call sites — `equal_obj` exists as a method but with different signature, `raw_arith` is a free function.

Let me first add the dependency.

172	/// Attempts raw (no-metamethod) arithmetic on two Lua values.
173	/// Writes the result to `res` and returns `true` on success, `false` if the
174	/// operation cannot be performed with the given types (caller should invoke
175	/// a metamethod instead).

delta.text: Done

. Report:

**lua-code type-vocabulary reconcile**

- **FAI
L violations before → after:** 1 → 0 (the `struct LuaState` duplicate at `codegen.rs:204
`). Remaining lua-code rows in the audit are all WARN (audit-m
ode) and pre-existing: a placeholder `struct LuaProto` at `codegen.rs:51`, plus other crates du

plicating lua-code-owned `OpCode`/`Instruction` (not in this scope).
- **Cargo deps added:** `
lua-vm.workspace = true` in `crates/lua-code/Cargo.toml`. No cycle (lua-vm does

not depend on lua-code).
- **todo!() stubs introduced:** 1 — `todo!("phase-
b-reconcile: raw_arith free-fn signature mismatch")` at the

`constfolding` call site.
- **Unresolved / notable:**
  1. `state.ra
w_arith(op, &v1, &v2)?` — canonical impl is a free function `lua_vm
::object::raw_arith(state, ArithOp, &, &, &mut L
uaValue) -> Result<bool, _>`, completely different shape from the old method stub. Stubbed with `todo!`; re
conciling would require adopting `ArithOp` throughout codegen and threading an out-param, which

is a real port task, not a swap.
  2. `state.equal_ob
j(None, …)` survived the swap unchanged — canonical signature uses `Option<&LuaValue

>` for the ctx arg, and `None` works under both shapes, so no call-site edit was needed.

ate still compiles (`cargo check -p lua-code` clean, 127 pre-existing warnings). PORT STATUS trailer tod
o count refreshed (15 → 13). No edits outside `crates/lua-code/`.

content.text:
Done. Report:

**lua-code type-vocabulary reconcile**

- **FAIL violations before → after:** 1 → 0 (the `struct LuaState` duplicate at `codegen.rs:204`). Remaining lua-code rows in the audit are all WARN (audit-mode) and pre-existing: a placeholder `struct LuaProto` at `codegen.rs:51`, plus other crates duplicating lua-code-owned `OpCode`/`Instruction` (not in this scope).
- **Cargo deps added:** `lua-vm.workspace = true` in `crates/lua-code/Cargo.toml`. No cycle (lua-vm does not depend on lua-code).
- **todo!() stubs introduced:** 1 — `todo!("phase-b-reconcile: raw_arith free-fn signature mismatch")` at the `constfolding` call site.
- **Unresolved / notable:**
  1. `state.raw_arith(op, &v1, &v2)?` — canonical impl is a free function `lua_vm::object::raw_arith(state, ArithOp, &, &, &mut LuaValue) -> Result<bool, _>`, completely different shape from the old method stub. Stubbed with `todo!`; reconciling would require adopting `ArithOp` throughout codegen and threading an out-param, which is a real port task, not a swap.
  2. `state.equal_obj(None, …)` survived the swap unchanged — canonical signature uses `Option<&LuaValue>` for the ctx arg, and `None` works under both shapes, so no call-site edit was needed.

Crate still compiles (`cargo check -p lua-code` clean, 127 pre-existing warnings). PORT STATUS trailer todo count refreshed (15 → 13). No edits outside `crates/lua-code/`.
